description: A field of gyronny of black and white (the historic flag of Lisbon), with the coat of arms of Ceuta charged in the centre.
history: Ceuta, a Spanish autonomous city on the North African coast of the Strait of Gibraltar, has used a flag based on the medieval ensign of Lisbon since 1415, when it was conquered by King John I of Portugal. The gyronny of black and white reflects this Portuguese heritage; Ceuta passed to the Spanish Crown by the Treaty of Lisbon in 1668. The flag with the coat of arms was officially confirmed by the Statute of Autonomy of 1995.

features.symbolism: Gyronny of black and white in five points (the historic flag of Lisbon, dating from Ceuta's period of Portuguese rule), with the coat of arms of the city centred on the field.
the city's heraldic arms, quartered with the Royal Arms of Portugal and a tower over a saltire, recalling Ceuta's Portuguese origins (1415-1668) and its later incorporation into the Spanish Crown.
